registry.description: Imagination is the inner capacity to form mental images of what is not immediately present — to picture, conceive, and give form to what does not yet exist or what cannot be directly seen. The Hebrew vocabulary here covers figures and reflections; the Greek covers reflection and contemplation. In Scripture imagination is treated with some suspicion when it runs unchecked (the imaginations of the heart are evil continually, Genesis 6) but it is also foundational to prayer, prophecy, and the capacity to hope for what is not yet seen.
registry.inference_note: Imagination as a faculty of the mind — the capacity to form mental images and conceive possibilities — is ABSENT as a direct biblical term. Scripture addresses imagination primarily in the context of corrupt mental fabrications rather than as a neutral creative faculty.

terms.lsj_entry:
ἐνθῡμ-έομαι, 
future -ήσομαι[Refs 5th c.BC+], later -ηθήσομαι [Refs 1st c.AD+]: aorist ἐνεθυμήθην [Refs 5th c.BC+]: perfect ἐντεθύμημαι [Refs 5th c.BC+]: pluperfect ἐνετεθύμητο [Refs 5th c.BC+]:—lay to heart, ponder, ἤτοι κρίνομέν γε ἢ ἐνθυμούμεθα ὀρθῶς τὰ πράγματα [Refs 5th c.BC+]; ἐ. καὶ λογίζεσθαι frequently joined in [Refs 4th c.BC+]
__b with genitive, ἐνθυμεῖσθαί τινος think much or deeply of, τοῦ θανόντος [Refs 7th c.BC+]
__c followed by a relative, ἐ. ὅτι.. notice or consider that.., [Refs 5th c.BC+]; ὡς.. how.., [Refs 5th c.BC+]
__d with participle, οὐκ ἐντεθύμηται ἐπαιρόμενος is not conscious that he is becoming excited, [Refs 5th c.BC+]
__2 take to heart, be concerned or angry at, τι [Refs 5th c.BC+]: absolutely, to be concerned, [Refs 5th c.BC+]
__3 form a plan, κράτιστος ἐνθυμηθῆναι [Refs 5th c.BC+]; take care, see to it, ἐ. ἵνα μηθεὶς ἀδικῇ [Refs 3rd c.BC+]
__4 infer, conclude, τί οὖν ἐκ τούτων.. ἐνθυμεῖσθαι δε; [Refs 4th c.BC+]
__II active, ἐνθυμέω [Refs 5th c.BC+]; ἐνθυμέομαι, in passive sense, to be in a person's thoughts, to be desired, κρατεῖν τῶν ἐνθυμουμένων [Refs 2nd c.AD+]: perfect [Refs 5th c.BC+]

senses.sense_text:
to think, ponder, reflect 
to revolve thoroughly in the mind, consider carefully, ponder, reflect, Acts 10:19*

verses.verse_text: Mat 1:20 But as he considered these things , behold , an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream , saying , “ Joseph , son of David , do not fear to take Mary as your wife , for that which is conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit .
Mat 9:4 But Jesus , knowing their thoughts , said , “ Why do you think evil in your hearts ?
Act 10:19 And while Peter was pondering the vision , the Spirit said to him , “ Behold , three men are looking for you .

verses.verse_text: Mat 9:4 But Jesus , knowing their thoughts , said , “ Why do you think evil in your hearts ?
Mat 12:25 Knowing their thoughts , he said to them , “ Every kingdom divided against itself is laid waste , and no city or house divided against itself will stand .
Act 17:29 Being then God’s offspring , we ought not to think that the divine being is like gold or silver or stone , an image formed by the art and imagination of man .

Heb 4:12 For the word of God is living and active , sharper than any two-edged sword , piercing to the division of soul and of spirit , of joints and of marrow , and discerning the thoughts and intentions of the heart .

verses.verse_text: Lev 26:1 “You shall not make idols for yourselves or erect an image or pillar , and you shall not set up a figured stone in your land to bow down to it, for I am the Lord your God .
Num 33:52 then you shall drive out all the inhabitants of the land from before you and destroy all their figured stones and destroy all their metal images and demolish all their high places .
Psa 73:7 Their eyes swell out through fatness ; their hearts overflow with follies .
Pro 18:11 A rich man’s wealth is his strong city , and like a high wall in his imagination .
Pro 25:11 A word fitly spoken is like apples of gold in a setting of silver .

Eze 8:12 Then he said to me, “ Son of man , have you seen what the elders of the house of Israel are doing in the dark , each in his room of pictures ? For they say , ‘The Lord does not see us, the Lord has forsaken the land .’”
